title: quassia amara
intro: Quassia amara, also known as quassia or amargo, is a tropical plant native to the Caribbean and Central America, belonging to the Rutaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects. The health benefits of Quassia amara include treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, as well as alleviating symptoms of fever and rheumatism. Quassia amara can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or extract, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including skin infections and respiratory issues.
